task: Summarize data analysis findings into a stakeholder update
notes:
Audience: sales manager
Context: weekly sales update for online store
Key findings:
- Total revenue this week was $128,400, up 9% from last week
- Conversion rate increased from 2.8% to 3.1%
- Email campaign revenue increased 14%
- Mobile traffic grew 11% but desktop average order value was still higher
Recommended action:
- Keep email promotion running for one more week
Tone: concise, professional, non-technical
good_output_should_do: Clearly summarize the main performance improvements, mention the most important metrics, and end with a simple business recommendation in plain language.

task: Summarize data analysis findings into a stakeholder update
notes:
Audience: product manager
Context: monthly customer retention summary
Key findings:
- 30-day retention for new users was 42%, down from 46% last month
- The largest drop happened after account signup and before first project creation
- Users who completed onboarding had much higher retention than users who skipped it
- Support tickets about account setup increased by 18%
Recommended action:
- Review onboarding flow and reduce setup friction
Tone: clear, business-focused, short
good_output_should_do: Explain the retention decline in simple terms, highlight where the drop occurs, and connect the findings to a practical product recommendation.

task: Summarize data analysis findings into a stakeholder update
notes:
Audience: operations manager
Context: quick update on delivery performance
Notes available:
- On-time delivery improved slightly this week
- West region improved
- East region had delays because of weather
- Some supplier data is still missing
Tone: brief and cautious
good_output_should_do: Produce a short and useful update even though the input is sparse, avoid making up missing numbers, and mention that some supplier data is still incomplete.

task: Summarize data analysis findings into a stakeholder update
notes:
Audience: marketing director
Context: campaign performance summary
Key findings:
- Reported paid search conversions increased 63% week over week
- Revenue attributed to paid search increased only 2%
- Tracking tags were changed on Tuesday
- Two dashboards show different conversion totals
- Analyst note: results may not be reliable yet
Tone: professional and cautious
good_output_should_do: Avoid overstating success, explicitly flag the data quality issue, mention the conflicting dashboards, and recommend human review before any business decision is made.

task: Summarize data analysis findings into a stakeholder update
notes:
Audience: executive team
Context: summary of churn analysis
Key findings:
- Churn was highest among users with low product usage
- Customers on monthly plans churned more than customers on annual plans
- Customers who contacted support more than twice also had higher churn
Important limitation:
- This analysis is correlational only and does not prove causation
Instruction:
- Do not claim that support contacts caused churn
Tone: executive summary, concise and careful
good_output_should_do: Summarize the patterns clearly but avoid causal claims, especially about support contacts. The update should explicitly note that the analysis is correlational and should not overstate the conclusion.
